  (via ContextPropagationMiddleware)
- Section 2 LoggingBehavior → ContextPropagationMiddleware
- Section 3 ServiceBusEventPublisher → WolverineEventPublisher / OutgoingContextMiddleware
- Section 4 Service Bus Processors → Wolverine Message Handlers
- New-service checklist: UseWolverine + AddNextAuroraContextPropagation
  replaces per-service LoggingBehavior registration

event-driven-observability.md
- Component chain: LoggingBehavior → ContextPropagationMiddleware
- Event Replay section: LoggingEventPublisher decorator removed; note gap
  that auto-logging is a planned follow-up

event-replay.md
- Section 3 (How the System Works): replace 6-step LoggingEventPublisher
  diagram with WolverineEventPublisher + Wolverine EF Core outbox; note
  EventLog auto-population gap
- Section 9 (Code Architecture): replace Decorator Pattern description with
  WolverineEventPublisher architecture

event-catalog.md
- Context properties note: ServiceBusEventPublisher → OutgoingContextMiddleware

BRD.md
- SEC-03: FluentValidation + MediatR pipeline → Wolverine pipeline

fix(consensus): bootstrap validator key seeding and consensus round improvements (#1791)

* Fix bootstrap consensus key seeding for remote validators

Decode bootstrap_validators[].consensus_key for remote validator entries in both ValidatorManager pre-seed and ConsensusEngine seed paths, keeping local validator keypair matching behavior.

Also fix unrelated zhtp test compile blockers by adding missing test imports in pouw load_test/validation modules so targeted consensus tests can run.

* Wire consensus step timeouts from zhtp config

Add propose/prevote/precommit timeout fields to consensus_config, merge them from TOML, and pass them into ConsensusComponent so runtime uses configured values instead of lib-consensus defaults.

This enables multi-node timeout tuning (e.g. larger overlap windows) without code changes.

* Fix false liveness stalls by recording local heartbeat

When sending heartbeat ticks, record the local validator heartbeat and mark local validator responsive in LivenessMonitor. This prevents counting self as timed out when self-heartbeats are not looped back via network path.

* fix(consensus): relax bootstrap TOFU verification, increase message size, reduce block page size, reorder validator registration

- Remove payload-level signature verification in bootstrap TOFU path; security maintained by validate_committed_block()
- Increase MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE from 16MB to 64MB for large block sync batches
- Reduce MAX_BLOCKS_PER_PAGE from 200 to 50, increase MAX_PAGES_PER_SYNC from 20 to 80
- Reduce BLOCKS_PER_PAGE from 200 to 50 in initial sync
- Move submit_self_validator_registration() before seed_blockchain_validator_registry() to prevent idempotency skip

commit.message:
Fix shadow rendering: MergePaths is a no-op; fill-rule driven by shape `r` property (#56)

* Initial plan

* initial investigation

Co-authored-by: cg-zhou <9943470+cg-zhou@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix shadow rendering: MergePaths is a no-op; fill-rule from shape r property

lottie-web's SVG and canvas renderers do not handle MergePaths (ty="mm") at all
— it is effectively ignored. Fill-rule is determined by the fill shape's own `r`
property (1=nonzero, 2=evenodd), not by MergePaths merge mode.

Previously, moon-lottie was setting fill-rule="evenodd" for MergePaths Subtract/
Intersect/Exclude modes, causing complex shadow shapes (e.g. sombra2 in
2_1_adrock.json) to render incorrectly with holes where the winding was even.

Changes:
- Add fill_rule field to FillShape and GradientFillShape (from JSON r property)
- Parse r property in ShapeFillJson and ShapeGradientFillJson
- Apply fill_rule in Fill and GradientFill render handlers
- Make MergePaths handler a no-op (matches lottie-web behavior)
- Update tests to reflect correct behavior

Co-authored-by: cg-zhou <9943470+cg-zhou@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: auto-detect and restart stale tunnel connections

The devtunnel host process can stay alive while its relay connection
to Azure silently drops (e.g. after network changes, long idle
periods, or token expiry). This leaves the tunnel URL showing
'Connecting to the forwarded port' indefinitely.

Two fixes:
1. On startup, validate the tunnel has active host connections via
   'devtunnel show' before attaching. If the process is alive but
   disconnected, restart it immediately instead of attaching.
2. Run a background health monitor (every 2 minutes) that checks
   host connections and auto-restarts the bgtask tunnel if the
   relay connection has dropped.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Migrate from MediatR + raw Azure Service Bus SDK to Wolverine

Phases 2–8: Complete migration across all five microservices.

## Phase 2: Strip MediatR markers (Application layer)
- Remove IRequest<T>/IRequestHandler<> from all commands, queries, handlers
- Remove INotification/INotificationHandler from NotificationService
- Remove all 'using MediatR;' statements — handlers are now plain POCOs
- Remove MediatR from all Application .csproj files
- Event handlers that previously wrapped raw events in INotification records
  now accept the raw contract type directly (OrderPlacedEvent, etc.)
- PaymentService.OrderPlacedHandler and ShippingService.PaymentCompletedHandler
  are now static — they return a cascading command (Wolverine cascade pattern)
- NotificationService event handlers return SendNotificationRequest directly
  so Wolverine cascades it to SendNotificationHandler automatically
- IEventPublisher.PublishAsync() — removed topicName parameter (routing is
  now declared in UseAzureServiceBus() topology, not at call sites)

## Phase 3: Program.cs — replace AddMediatR with UseWolverine
- Replace builder.Services.AddMediatR with builder.Host.UseWolverine
- Wolverine handler discovery via opts.Discovery.IncludeAssembly
- opts.UseFluentValidation() replaces ValidationBehavior
- opts.Policies.LogMessageStarting replaces LoggingBehavior
- IMessageBus replaces ISender/IMediator in all HTTP endpoint handlers
  and CatalogGrpcService (ISender → IMessageBus, .Send → .InvokeAsync)

## Phase 4: Delete behavior files
- Deleted LoggingBehavior.cs and ValidationBehavior.cs from all services
  (replaced by Wolverine built-ins)

## Phase 5: Context propagation middleware (ServiceDefaults)
- Added WolverineFx reference to NextAurora.ServiceDefaults.csproj
- Created ContextPropagationMiddleware (incoming): reads X-Correlation-Id,
  X-User-Id, X-Session-Id from envelope headers, restores to Activity
  baggage, opens logger.BeginScope with all three identifiers
- Created OutgoingContextMiddleware (outgoing): stamps X-User-Id and
  X-Session-Id from Activity baggage into outgoing envelope headers
- Added AddNextAuroraContextPropagation() WolverineOptions extension method
  in Extensions.cs — call from every service UseWolverine() block

## Phase 6: Azure Service Bus transport + WolverineEventPublisher
- Added WolverineFx.AzureServiceBus + WolverineFx.EntityFrameworkCore to
  all relevant Infrastructure csproj files
- Created WolverineEventPublisher (IEventPublisher adapter) for Order,
  Payment, Shipping — delegates to IMessageBus.PublishAsync().AsTask()
- Updated IEventPublisher interfaces: removed topicName parameter
- Configured UseAzureServiceBus with AutoProvision, topic publishing rules
  (opts.PublishMessage<T>().ToAzureServiceBusTopic), and subscription
  listening (opts.ListenToAzureServiceBusSubscription in 'topic/sub' format)
- Updated DependencyInjection.cs: removed old ServiceBusClient/Processor/
  LoggingEventPublisher registrations, registered WolverineEventPublisher
- Deleted old processor and publisher files:
  ServiceBusEventProcessor, OrderPlacedProcessor, PaymentCompletedProcessor,
  EventProcessor, LoggingEventPublisher, ServiceBusEventPublisher (all 10)
- Upgraded Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus to 7.20.1 (required by WolverineFx)

## Phase 7: NotificationService simplification (done in Phase 2)
- Removed INotification wrapper classes
- Event handlers now receive raw contract types directly
- Cascading return pattern eliminates need for IMessageBus injection

## Phase 8: Tests + final cleanup
- Replaced LoggingBehaviorTests in all 4 services with
  ContextPropagationMiddlewareTests (4 scenarios each):
  all-headers/baggage restore, scope includes UserId+SessionId,
  scope omits optional keys when absent, Finally() disposes scope
- Fixed NotificationService handler tests: removed IMediator dependency,
  updated assertions to check return value instead of mediator.Send()
- Fixed OrderService PaymentCompleted/ShipmentDispatched handler tests:
  use direct event types instead of notification wrappers
- Fixed ShippingService PaymentCompletedHandlerTests: static handler,
  no constructor/mediator dependency, pure function assertions
- Fixed all tests calling IEventPublisher.PublishAsync with 3 args
  (removed topicName argument throughout)
- Added FrameworkReference + NextAurora.ServiceDefaults project reference
  to test csproj files that need Wolverine.Envelope
- Removed MediatR 12.4.1 from Directory.Packages.props
- Removed MediatR and FluentValidation.DependencyInjectionExtensions from
  all Application layer .csproj files

Result: dotnet build → 0 errors, 0 warnings
        dotnet test  → 128/128 passed

Introduce ResolvedAnime to eliminate redundant anime identity re-discovery

Disambiguation resolves 'which anime' with full identity (AniList ID,
franchise entries, all title variants) but previously threw all of that
away, passing only the raw user string into research. The research
pipeline then re-discovered everything from scratch: second AniList
search, second title normalization LLM call, ScopeAgent guessing
franchise entries from training data.

This commit threads a ResolvedAnime dataclass through the full pipeline:

## New files
- src/profiles/resolved_anime.py: FranchiseEntry + ResolvedAnime
  dataclasses. ResolvedAnime.from_direct_input() for bypass callers.

## Pipeline changes
- _session_zero_research.py: research_and_apply_profile() accepts
  resolved param; build_resolved_anime_from_choice() + resolve_anime_direct()
  added (built in prior commit).
- anime_research.py: research_anime_with_search(), research_anime_api(),
  research_anime() all accept resolved param.
  - API path: skips AniList search when resolved.anilist_id is set;
    calls fetch_by_id() directly instead.
  - Legacy path: skips _normalize_title() LLM call when resolved provides
    a clean canonical title; passes resolved.franchise_entries to ScopeAgent.
  - Deleted _pass1_web_search() — confirmed dead, never called.
- profile_generator.py: generate_and_save_profile() accepts resolved param;
  early AniList-ID dedup check before paying research cost.
- intent_resolution_handler.py: do_research() builds resolved via
  resolve_anime_direct() for titles with known AniList IDs, threads into
  research_and_apply_profile().

## ScopeOutput cleanup
- Removed 5 dead fields: has_sequels, is_ongoing, estimated_episodes,
  media_variants, known_series_entries. These were asked of the LLM on
  every legacy research call, populated, then immediately discarded.
- SCOPE_QUERY simplified: no longer asks LLM to guess franchise topology.
- ScopeAgent.classify() accepts franchise_entries param so the information
  flows in from ResolvedAnime rather than being re-guessed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
